question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted transaction that causes Math.addExact() to throw ArithmeticException during transfersBalance.merge() by injecting a crypto transfer with Long.MAX_VALUE amount, causing reconciliation to halt with FAILURE_UNKNOWN and masking a real balance discrepancy that represents direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcileTokenTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token transfer with amount Long.MAX_VALUE to trigger Math.addExact() overflow in tokenBalances.merge(), causing an ArithmeticException that halts reconciliation entirely and prevents detection of a concurrent token balance theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS invariant check to throw ReconciliationException by injecting a balance file where the sum of account_balance rows does not equal FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS, thereby halting all future reconciliation runs and permanently masking ongoing fund theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ACCUMULATE RemediationStrategy by injecting a crafted transfer that creates a persistent balance discrepancy, causing reconcileTransfers() to log the error but continue processing with the corrupted previous.getBalances() map, permanently masking a real fund loss across all subsequent reconciliation windows?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that reconciliationJob.setConsensusTimestamp() is only called when !reconciliationJob.hasErrors() to cause the reconciliation checkpoint to stall at a specific timestamp, forcing repeated re-processing of the same window and never advancing past a balance discrepancy that hides a fund theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the errata filter (errata <> 'DELETE') in CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY to inject a crypto transfer with a non-DELETE errata value (e.g., 'INSERT') that is included in the transfer sum, causing the reconciliation to accept a fabricated balance increase that masks a real fund theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: TOKEN_TRANSFER_QUERY] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any errata filter in TOKEN_TRANSFER_QUERY (unlike CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY which filters errata <> 'DELETE') to inject a deleted/erroneous token transfer that is still included in the token balance sum, causing reconcileTokenTransfers() to accept a false token balance and mask a token theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNextBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the accountBalanceFile.getTimeOffset() value used in BalanceSnapshot.getTimestamp() to shift the consensus_timestamp range passed to CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY, causing transfers from outside the intended window to be included or excluded, resulting in a false reconciliation pass that hides a fund theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNextBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an AccountBalanceFile with consensusTimestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 so that getTimestamp() computes Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 + timeOffset and overflows, causing CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY to receive a negative toTimestamp and return no transfers, making reconciliation silently pass despite a real balance discrepancy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where reconciliationJob.getCount() % 100 == 0 causes reconciliationJobRepository.save() to persist a SUCCESS checkpoint just before a balance discrepancy is detected in the next iteration, so that after a crash-restart the reconciliation resumes past the theft window and never re-checks it?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: equals()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that equals() uses != for long primitive comparison but iterates only over entries present in each map, so that if an account with a zero balance is present in one map but absent in the other (default 0L via getOrDefault), the comparison incorrectly returns true and a real balance discrepancy (fund theft) goes undetected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crypto transfer with a negative amount equal in magnitude to a legitimate transfer, causing transfersBalance.merge() with Math.addExact to produce a net zero for a specific accountId, making reconcileTransfers() pass even though funds were actually moved out of that account without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getLatestJob()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Math.max(startDate, consensusTimestamp) logic in getLatestJob() by causing the reconciliationJobRepository to return a stale consensusTimestamp that is less than startDate, forcing reconciliation to restart from startDate and skip a theft window that was already partially processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ACCUMULATE strategy's balance carry-forward logic (previous = Optional.of(current.get().toBuilder().balances(previous.get().getBalances())...)) to accumulate a growing balance discrepancy across multiple windows, where each individual window passes reconciliation but the total drift represents a large undetected fund theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ACCUMULATE strategy where reconciliationJob.setError() appends errors via StringUtils.joinWith but reconciliationJob.setConsensusTimestamp() is never called on error, causing the job to process all remaining balance files without ever advancing the checkpoint, so a theft in the first window is never re-examined after restart?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user inject duplicate account_id rows in the account_balance table for the same consensusTimestamp, causing balances.put() to silently overwrite the first entry and total.addAndGet() to double-count the balance, making the FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS check pass with incorrect per-account balances that hide a fund theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTokenBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user inject duplicate (account_id, token_id) rows in the token_balance table for the same consensusTimestamp, causing balances.put() to silently overwrite the first entry and produce incorrect token balances that make reconcileTokenTransfers() pass despite a real token theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized keyword on reconcile() combined with the @Scheduled cron trigger to cause a missed execution (if the previous run is still in progress), creating a gap in reconciliation coverage where a fund theft occurring during the skipped window is never detected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of small crypto transfers that cause transfersBalance to grow to millions of entries, exhausting JVM heap memory during reconcileCryptoTransfers() and causing an OutOfMemoryError that halts reconciliation with FAILURE_UNKNOWN, masking a concurrent fund theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcileTokenTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user create a large number of unique (account_id, token_id) pairs in token_transfer to cause tokenBalances HashMap to exhaust memory during reconcileTokenTransfers(), triggering an OutOfMemoryError that prevents detection of a token balance theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNextBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an AccountBalanceFile with a consensusTimestamp of 0, causing fromTimestamp = 0 + 1 - 1 = 0 in the recordFileRepository.findNextBetween(timestamp - 1L, Long.MAX_VALUE) call, and if timestamp is 0 then timestamp - 1L underflows to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the record file lookup to return no results and the snapshot to be built with an empty/wrong record file reference?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: BALANCE_QUERY] Can an unprivileged external user inject a row into account_balance with a negative balance value, causing total.addAndGet() to produce a sum less than FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS, triggering a ReconciliationException that halts reconciliation and prevents detection of a real fund theft occurring in the same time window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Maps.difference() call in reconcileTransfers() to cause a very large difference object to be formatted into an error string via String.format(), consuming excessive memory and causing an OutOfMemoryError that prevents the error from being persisted and masks the balance discrepancy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the reconciliation loop only saves progress every 100 iterations (reconciliationJob.getCount() % 100 == 0) to cause a crash between saves, forcing reconciliation to restart from up to 99 balance files earlier and re-process a window where a theft was already detected but the error was not yet persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getLatestJob()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reconciliationJobRepository.findLatest() returning a job with status SUCCESS but a consensusTimestamp that is far in the past, causing reconciliation to re-process a large historical window and potentially re-trigger false positives that suppress detection of a current fund theft?
